methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Asterra is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $20M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of five public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private peers in the same sector, with particular focus on four same-stage or adjacent-stage private companies: Aalyria ($1.3B Series B), ClearSpace ($400M Series A), Hubble Network ($400M Series B), and Sift ($274M Series B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $20M raised proxy, implying a $40M valuation. The peer-median method yields $400M, derived from the median valuation across the four private comparables, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Asterra's revenue is not disclosed; the sector median P/S of 53.2x across public Space & Aerospace names would require revenue visibility to be actionable.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $34M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $220M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around Asterra's product maturity, customer traction, and technical defensibility relative to peers. Stage-progression favors the lower bound, while peer-median analysis—particularly the $400M ClearSpace comparable—anchors the upper half. Key unknowns include revenue run rate, customer concentration, and near-term milestone probability.
